answer:
**Chapter 8** of Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation, Vol. I* is dedicated to criticizing the system that makes a positive religion the exclusive foundation of morality and laws.[^1]

The chapter title itself states: "Of the system that makes a positive religion the exclusive foundation of morality and laws, and of the influence of this system on civilization."[^1] Comte argues that this approach excludes observation of facts and reasoning, relying solely on precepts attributed to a supernatural being, which hinders progress by preventing analysis of consequences in this life.[^2] He notes its historical applications (e.g., admiration for Mohammed's institutions) and warns it leads to stagnation, hypocrisy, and obstacles to enlightenment, contrasting it with systems allowing rational inquiry.[^3] This critique aligns directly with the query, as no other chapter in Vol. I matches this focus based on the table of contents.[^4]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. I*, [Chapter 8](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/08-du-systeme-qui-fait-d-une-religion-positive-le-fon/translation/).

[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. I*, [p-a28373](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/08-du-systeme-qui-fait-d-une-religion-positive-le-fon/translation/#p-a28373).

[^3]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation, Vol. I*, [Chapter 8](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/08-du-systeme-qui-fait-d-une-religion-positive-le-fon/translation/).

[^4]: Chapters list for *Traité de Législation, Vol. I*.
